title: Two-Axis Tracking Solar Collectors Simulation with Python
author: Jensen, Sifnaios, et al.

steps.title: Method details
steps.instruction: Two-axis trackers are typically used with highly concentrating solar energy technologies due to their ability to always orient the collecting surface normal to the sun. However, two-axis trackers typically experience greater shading losses than fixed-tilt collectors [2]. Failing to account for shading by neighboring collectors, referred to as self-shading, results in an overestimation of power production at low solar elevation angles. Typical annual shading losses are between 1% for sparse field layouts and 6% for dense field layouts [3]. As accurate yield estimations are critical to obtaining financing for solar energy projects, it is imperative that validated methods are available for accounting for shading.
